title: For $6.5m, this Connecticut inn comes with a liquor license in trendy Mystic
author: Regina Cole
bullets: The Captain's Mansion inn, located in Mystic, Connecticut, is for sale at $6.5 million.
This luxury property includes a liquor license, eight bedrooms, and features historic Italianate architecture.
The 10,335 square foot inn comes fully furnished, offering the option to continue operating as a hotel.

title: Fishing crew rescued near Gloucester after boat runs aground amid harsh seas
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two crew members rescued after 40-foot fishing boat ran aground near Atlantic Road, Gloucester on Saturday night.
Rescue involved Gloucester Fire, Police, Harbormaster, and U.S. Coast Guard amid harsh seas and increasing hull damage.
The crew members were unharmed after using rescue lines to reach dry land, officials confirmed.

title: The Rafael Devers trade is already haunting the Red Sox in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox are struggling early in the 2026 season with a 2-7 record after the Rafael Devers trade.
Boston received Jordan Hicks, Kyle Harrison, James Tibbs, and Jose Bello in the trade; only Bello remains with the team.
Kyle Harrison and James Tibbs are performing well with new teams, highlighting Boston's poor asset management decisions.

title: Why it might be time for a slumping Bruins team to call up James Hagens
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins have scored only three goals in their last three games, facing a slump just before playoffs.
James Hagens, a forward prospect currently in AHL, could be called up to boost Bruins' weakening offense.
Hagens, drafted 7th overall in 2025, shows potential to aid the Bruins with his passing and power-play skills.

title: Tatum, Celtics pull away late to beat Raptors, move closer to 2-seed: 6 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: The Boston Celtics defeated the Toronto Raptors 113-95 with strong performances from Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown.
Neemias Queta's standout play helped the team, recording 18 points, seven rebounds, and three blocks against the Raptors.
Boston Celtics are moving closer to securing the 2-seed in the playoffs with four games remaining in the regular season.

title: New Bedford man gets decades-long prison sentence for raping young relative
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Daniel Turcios Torres, 46, sentenced to 36-45 years for repeatedly raping a relative from 2019.
Charges included multiple counts of child rape, indecent assault, and witness intimidation, according to prosecutors.
Victim reported the abuse to New Bedford police in July 2024, after initially being intimidated to stay silent.

title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes, 23, from Boxford, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her late mother.
Her mother died of Stage 4 brain cancer after receiving care at Massachusetts General Hospital.
Oakes, now a nurse, runs to support MGH and works in oncology to help cancer patients recover.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police fatally shot a man allegedly experiencing a mental health crisis while wielding a sword near Northeastern University.
The suspect stabbed a police officer and knocked an EMS worker to the ground before being shot by officers.
Boston police and the Suffolk County District Attorney are investigating; multiple officers and EMS workers suffered non-life-threatening injuries.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois, 23, from Hudson, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer.
Dubois aims to support the Cancer Care Equity program at MGH, assisting families with expenses during cancer treatments.
She qualified for the marathon through the 2025 Lake Erie Marathon and is running with the Massachusetts General Hospital team.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A resident died and two others were hospitalized after a house fire on Glendale Ave in Brockton early Saturday.
The fire was intense, with heaviest flames on the second floor, appearing not suspicious but cause still unknown.
Firefighters found a deceased woman in her 40s; the exact cause of the fire is under investigation by authorities.
